originalTitle: Police fire shots in air to disperse angry crowds at DR Congo Ebola treatment centre
title: Helping Friends Stay Healthy in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, super friends! It’s your kid reporter, here to tell you about something important happening far away in a beautiful country called DR Congo. It's a place in Africa, just like many of our homes! People there are working together to stay safe and healthy from a tiny germ.

What is a Germ Called Ebola?

Imagine a tiny, tiny thing you cannot see, like a little invisible bug. We call it a 'germ'. Most germs are harmless, but some can make us feel sick. One of these special germs is called Ebola. When someone gets this Ebola germ, they might feel very tired, have a fever, or a tummy ache. It’s like a very, very strong cold or flu. Did you know? Ebola is a very rare germ! It's not like the common cold that many people get. It only happens in certain places. It's important to remember that doctors and nurses know a lot about how to help people who get sick with Ebola. They work super hard to make everyone feel better!

Special Places to Get Better

When someone gets sick with the Ebola germ, they need special care. There are special places, a bit like hospitals, just for people with Ebola. We call them 'treatment centres'. In these centres, doctors and nurses wear special suits to keep themselves safe while they help. They give medicine and make sure sick people are comfortable and get good food. Did you know? The special suits that doctors and nurses wear are called Personal Protective Equipment, or PPE for short! They cover them from head to toe, like a superhero costume! Staying in these special centres is super important. It helps the sick person get better faster. It also helps stop the germ from spreading to other people in their family or village.

Why is it Important to Be Careful?

The Ebola germ can spread from someone who is very sick to another person if they touch them. It can also spread if you touch things a sick person has touched, like their clothes. This is why helpers ask families to follow special rules. For example, if someone passes away from Ebola, there are special, safe ways to bury them. This makes sure the germ does not spread and keeps everyone else safe. Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and clean water is one of the best ways to stop germs from spreading! We should all wash our hands often, especially before eating and after playing. It might be sad or confusing for families when these rules are needed, especially during a sad time. But the rules are always there to keep everyone safe and healthy.

Helpers in DR Congo

In DR Congo, there are many kind helpers. These are brave doctors, smart nurses, and also friendly people from the community. They all work together like a big team. Their biggest job is to make sure people know about Ebola. They teach everyone how to stay safe and what to do if someone gets sick. Sometimes, people can feel very worried or sad when a loved one is sick. It can be hard to understand why special rules are needed, especially for burial. Did you know? Many people in DR Congo are working to help their communities understand how to stay safe from Ebola! They are heroes in their own towns and villages, explaining things to everyone. The kind helpers try their best to talk to everyone. They explain in simple words why it's so important to follow the safety steps. They want to make sure everyone understands how to stay safe and healthy.

Working Together for a Healthy Community

Sometimes, when people are scared or sad, they might not agree with the helpers right away. They might want to do things their own way, which can be risky for spreading germs. But the helpers are always there to explain and protect everyone. They want to make sure the Ebola germ stops spreading completely, so no one else gets sick. It’s like when your teacher asks you to follow rules in the classroom. The rules are there to keep everyone safe and happy! It's the same in the community. Did you know? When everyone works together and listens to the helpers, it makes it much easier to stop germs like Ebola from spreading! Teamwork is super powerful and helps everyone. In DR Congo, people are learning more and more about how to live safely. They are learning to trust the doctors and nurses, and how to keep their homes and villages healthy.

How Can We Help?

Even though we might be far away, we can still help! We can learn about being healthy and strong. We can wash our hands often. We can be kind to everyone. And we can listen to our parents and teachers about staying safe and well, just like the people in DR Congo listen to their helpers. Did you know? Thinking positive thoughts for people far away is a way to send them good energy! We can wish everyone in DR Congo health and happiness. We can also remember that there are brave doctors, nurses, and community leaders. They are working hard every single day to protect everyone. They are helping to make sure that the people of DR Congo can stay strong and healthy. It's all about working together, just like good friends! Let’s always remember to be kind, be safe, and learn all we can about staying healthy! Hurray for healthy friends everywhere!
summary: Kind helpers in DR Congo are working hard to teach everyone how to stay safe and healthy from a tiny germ called Ebola, by using special care centres and following important rules.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how people can work together, listen to helpers, and learn to stay safe and healthy from germs, so everyone can live happily in their communities.

originalTitle: Kenya police shake up president's protection team after security breach
title: Kenya police shake up president's protection team after security breach
content: A man managed to evade security and embrace William Ruto before being wrestled to the ground.

originalTitle: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
title: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
content: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

originalTitle: Starbucks’ Korean sales fall after backlash to ‘Tank Day’ ad campaign
title: A Big Lesson from a Coffee Shop Far Away!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter, Jambo Joy, bringing you exciting stories from across Africa and the world! Today, we have a very important story that teaches us about being kind and thinking carefully, even for big companies. It happened very far away, in a country called Korea. Are you ready?

Meet Starbucks: A Shop with Yummy Drinks!

Starbucks is a very famous shop that sells delicious coffee and other yummy drinks. It's like a special place where grown-ups go to sip warm drinks and chat with friends. They also have tasty snacks like cakes and cookies! You can find Starbucks shops in many, many countries all over the world, and even in some places right here in Africa! People love their sweet coffees, creamy milkshakes, and cold drinks. Did you know? Coffee beans actually grow on trees! Farmers pick them carefully, clean them, and then roast them until they smell amazing. Then, they grind them into a powder to make the coffee drinks your parents might enjoy!

What is an 'Ad Campaign'?

Imagine you have a lemonade stand in front of your house. To tell everyone about your super yummy lemonade, you might draw a colorful sign, right? You want people to know your lemonade is the best and invite them to try it! Well, big shops like Starbucks do something very similar. They make special pictures, fun videos, and clever messages to tell people about their drinks and snacks. This is called an 'ad campaign' or 'advertising'. It's like shouting, 'Hey, come try our new amazing drink!' but in a very smart and eye-catching way. Companies use ad campaigns to make people excited and want to buy their products. They put these ads on TV, on big posters in the street, on the radio, and on the internet. It's how they invite you to come and see what new and tasty things they have for sale.

A Campaign That Made People Sad

Now, here's where our story from faraway Korea gets a little serious. In Korea, a country very, very far from us across the big oceans, Starbucks made a new ad campaign. But this ad campaign, sadly, accidentally reminded many people of a very sad and difficult day in their country's long history. It brought back feelings of unhappiness and pain for a lot of people. It’s a bit like if someone told a joke that wasn't funny at all and actually made some of your friends feel bad and upset. That's not a good thing, right? Companies, just like people, need to be very, very careful with the words and pictures they choose. They need to stop and think about how what they say or show might make other people feel, especially when it comes to important memories. Did you know? Korea is a country in Asia, a huge continent very far away from Africa! It has its own unique delicious food, lively music, and special traditions. It gets very, very cold there in winter, much colder than in many sunny parts of Africa!

People Stopped Buying Coffee

When people in Korea saw the ad that made them sad, they got very upset. They felt that Starbucks didn't understand their feelings or respect their important history. So, what did many people decide to do? They decided not to go to Starbucks anymore for a while. They chose to buy their coffee and drinks from other shops instead, as a way to show they were unhappy. Imagine if you made a drawing for your friend, but it accidentally made them sad. They might not want to play with you for a little bit until you understand their feelings and say sorry. That's kind of what happened. Because fewer people were buying drinks, Starbucks saw a 'very significant' drop in their sales. 'Sales' means how many drinks and snacks they sell every day. If sales drop, it means they are selling much, much less than before, and the shop is not making as much money. Did you know? When you buy something from a shop, that's called a 'sale'! The shop makes money when they make a sale. If they don't sell many things, they don't make much money to pay for their shop and their workers.

A Big Lesson for Everyone

This story from faraway Korea teaches us a very important lesson, my friends. It teaches us that being kind, thoughtful, and respectful is important, not just for people talking to each other, but for big companies too! Even if you don't mean to, sometimes your words or pictures can accidentally make others feel bad or bring up sad memories. When you are creating something, like a drawing, or even just talking to your friends, it's always good to stop and think: 'How might this make someone else feel? Will it make them happy, or could it make them sad or upset?' If it might make them sad or upset, maybe it's better to change it or choose different words. Big companies like Starbucks are learning this important lesson too. They are learning that they need to listen carefully to their customers and try their best to understand their feelings and cultural memories. It’s very important to show respect for everyone's history and experiences, both happy and sad.

What Happened Next?

Starbucks' local operator, which is the group of people who run the Starbucks shops in Korea, saw that many people were unhappy and that fewer people were buying drinks. They understood that they had made a mistake with their ad campaign. We hope they will be more careful and thoughtful next time, and make sure their ads make people feel happy and welcome, not sad or upset. Did you know? Even big, famous companies can make mistakes sometimes! The most important thing is to learn from those mistakes and try to do much better next time. Just like when you accidentally spill juice, you clean it up and try to be more careful and thoughtful next time!

Being Kind is Always Best!

So, my lovely listeners, this story is a reminder for all of us, no matter where we live. Whether we are playing with our friends, drawing pictures for school, or even running a big shop, thinking about others' feelings is always the best way to be. Let's always try to be kind, thoughtful, and understanding. That way, we can all live in a happier world, full of smiles! This is Jambo Joy, signing off with a big smile and a wave! See you next time for more exciting news!
summary: Starbucks in Korea had a problem when an ad campaign accidentally made many people sad because it reminded them of a bad time in their country's history, causing fewer people to buy their drinks.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that even big companies need to be careful and kind with their messages, always thinking about how their words and pictures might make other people feel, just like we should in our own lives.
